jokes.text: I would fuck her. Damn! - You like that? - Fucking love that shit.
jokes.setup: Peaceful yoga class with spiritual instructor guiding meditation
jokes.punchline: Larry and Leon crudely objectifying the instructor during sacred practice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-driven cringe - Larry's inappropriate behavior in spiritual settings is his trademark. The juxtaposition of sacred yoga with crude commentary creates strong discomfort comedy.

jokes.text: I wanted to do the downward dog. That's my favorite one. - You love that downward dog. - I'm a big dog.
jokes.setup: Larry expressing disappointment about missing a yoga pose
jokes.punchline: Leon's double entendre about being 'a big dog' with sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual wordplay. Gets a mild laugh but relies on obvious double meaning without much cleverness.

jokes.text: Yeah, not a namaste guy. I don't participate in group activities. You know, I don't sing the birthday song.
jokes.setup: Yoga instructor confronting Larry about not participating in namaste
jokes.punchline: Larry's characteristically antisocial justification extending to birthday songs
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy. Larry's refusal to participate in social rituals is perfectly consistent and the birthday song comparison is unexpectedly relatable and specific.

jokes.text: Namaste means 'the light within me greets the light within you.' - There is no light within me, that's the only problem there.
jokes.setup: Instructor explaining the beautiful spiritual meaning of namaste
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact declaration of his spiritual emptiness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of spiritual platitudes. Larry's deadpan self-awareness about his lack of inner light is perfectly timed and devastatingly funny. The economy is perfect.

jokes.text: My third eye is watching. - That's not how the third eye works. - You know how the third eye works, huh? - I've studied it for many years, yes. - Is that so? Here, watch, here's what I think about the third eye. See? Look, I'm poking it.
jokes.setup: Larry skeptical about third eye concept, instructor claiming expertise
jokes.punchline: Larry literally poking where the third eye would be to prove it's not real
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry physical comedy - using literal action to debunk mystical concepts. The escalation from verbal skepticism to physical demonstration is very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I wouldn't do it to my two eyes, the real eyes. - Right, right. The third eye's real. - There's no... there's no imaginary eye there.
jokes.setup: Larry's logic about not poking his real eyes
jokes.punchline: Instructor's frustrated insistence that the imaginary eye is real
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the third eye argument with Larry's literal-minded logic clashing with spiritual beliefs.

jokes.text: It's really hot in here. - Well, it's hot yoga. - Does it have to be this hot?
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about temperature
jokes.punchline: Instructor stating the obvious that it's 'hot yoga' and Larry's continued obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Larry's complete disconnect from yoga culture. Sets up the heat motif.

jokes.text: No, I'm here because I have a bad hamstring. - If you're not gonna acknowledge the light within yourself, then you can leave. You can leave and you cannot come back. - Wow. That's very 'unyogi-ish' of you. - Get the fuck out.
jokes.setup: Larry reducing spiritual practice to physical therapy, instructor's ultimatum
jokes.punchline: Larry calling out the instructor's hypocrisy, leading to profane outburst
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing the yoga instructor's spiritual facade cracking. Larry's 'unyogi-ish' neologism is perfect, and the contrast between zen philosophy and 'get the fuck out' is brilliant.

jokes.text: You just made my day. Okay. Namaste!
jokes.setup: Larry being kicked out of yoga class
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically using namaste as he leaves, now that he's been expelled
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with ironic twist. Larry finally says namaste but only as a sarcastic goodbye. The timing and economy are excellent.

jokes.text: Guess whose number I got. - Yogi Tina? - Yeah. Nice! Guess who just threw me out of class and told me never to come back. - Yogi Tina? - Yeah, Yogi Tina.
jokes.setup: Leon excited about getting instructor's number
jokes.punchline: Same instructor kicked Larry out - parallel structure reveals the irony
jokes.explanation: Great structural joke using parallel reveals. The same woman is simultaneously Larry's nemesis and Leon's conquest, showing their different approaches to life.

jokes.text: I wouldn't say namaste. - Man, you gotta say namaste, man. - Oh, get the fuck out of here. That's so stupid.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he was kicked out
jokes.punchline: Leon's incredulous reaction and Larry's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Leon understands social expectations that Larry rejects, but the joke is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Car accident sequence with backup camera malfunction
jokes.setup: Larry backing up while Leon fiddles with radio
jokes.punchline: Predictable car accident from distraction and technology failure
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup. The backup camera failure is somewhat predictable and relies on technology malfunction rather than character-driven humor.

jokes.text: I follow the golden rule, okay? 'Do unto others as you'd have others do unto you.' - I follow the golden rule, too. My friend Jasper's golden. 'Fuck them or they'll fuck you.'
jokes.setup: Larry citing the traditional golden rule about leaving a note
jokes.punchline: Leon's completely different 'golden rule' from his friend Jasper
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the golden rule concept. Leon's interpretation through his friend Jasper shows the cultural divide and creates a memorable quote.

jokes.text: I would be a little concerned if it was my daughter. - Well, you know, there's no accounting for who's attracted to... look, I'm attracted to Jeff. Go figure that one.
jokes.setup: Larry expressing concern about someone's romantic choice
jokes.punchline: Susie's self-deprecating admission about being attracted to Jeff
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor from Susie about the mystery of attraction, using Jeff as the obvious example of inexplicable romantic choices.

jokes.text: Do-does she know about the fatwa? - Yeah, and guess what? That turned her on!
jokes.setup: Larry worried about his fatwa affecting dating prospects
jokes.punchline: The death threat actually making him more attractive to the woman
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd callback showing how Larry's dangerous situation becomes a dating asset. The dark comedy of religious death threats being sexy is perfectly surreal.

jokes.text: You are on an Uber rating, you're like a... a two. - A two? - On a good day. On a good day! - Fuck you! A two, okay? - Yeah. I'm a four, baby! I'm an Uber four.
jokes.setup: Susie insulting Larry's attractiveness using Uber rating system
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate defense of being a four, Susie's crushing 'on a good day' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Great use of modern technology metaphor for attractiveness. The escalating insults and Larry's pathetic self-defense create strong cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Let me tell you something, if I was a two, I would kill myself. I... I could not live on this planet as a two.
jokes.setup: Larry defending his attractiveness rating
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic declaration about preferring death to being a two
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry hyperbole - turning vanity into existential crisis. The dramatic overreaction to a low attractiveness rating is classically Larry.

jokes.text: Oh, hey. - How you doing? - I'm looking for Greg. - Ah, I'm Greg. - Oh, hey, L-Larry. Jeff's friend. - Oh! - We, uh... we talked on the phone. - On the phone, yeah, yeah. - How you doing? Nice to meet you. - Eh, you're black.
jokes.setup: Larry meeting Greg the mechanic for the first time
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate, inappropriate observation about Greg's race
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry cringe - stating the obvious inappropriately. The timing and Larry's matter-of-fact delivery make this maximally uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Just surprised Jeff didn't mention it. - Why would he have to mention it? - Eh, because most white people with black friends like to show off about it, that's all.
jokes.setup: Greg questioning why his race should be mentioned
jokes.punchline: Larry's uncomfortable social observation about white people showing off diversity
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary wrapped in cringe. Larry's observation about performative diversity is accurate but inappropriate to voice.

jokes.text: I'm not prejudiced in any way towards any group, except my own, of course.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to clarify he's not prejudiced
jokes.punchline: The exception being prejudice against his own group (Jews)
jokes.explanation: Clever self-aware humor about Jewish self-deprecation. The twist that Larry's only prejudice is against his own people is perfectly character-consistent.

jokes.text: Romanian Uber driver rating Larry as a two
jokes.setup: Larry asking for honest assessment of his attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Second confirmation that Larry is indeed a two, validating Susie's insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback that validates Susie's harsh assessment. The repeated confirmation of Larry's low attractiveness builds the running gag.

jokes.text: You're thinking, maybe, of mountain women. Okay? I am not talking about mou... I'm talking about lowland women. Women of the plains. The most beautiful in all of Europe.
jokes.setup: Larry insulting Romanian women's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Driver's incredibly specific geographic defense of Romanian beauty standards
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific absurdist defense. The distinction between mountain and lowland Romanian women is hilariously detailed and passionate.

jokes.text: you're from one of the most unattractive countries, you know, on the planet. It's all skewed 'cause all you've seen are unattractive people your entire life.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to defend his rating
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredibly offensive generalization about Romanians
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Larry behavior - his desperation to maintain his rating leads to offensive generalizations.

jokes.text: Next time, I won't leave a note. How 'bout that?
jokes.setup: Angry car owner berating Larry for leaving a note instead of staying
jokes.punchline: Larry's threat to be less considerate in the future
jokes.explanation: Great Larry logic - threatening to be less ethical when his ethics are unappreciated. Shows his petty but understandable reaction to ingratitude.

jokes.text: They try to get the penises in, and the pussy. Wet pussy, tight pussy, hot pussy. You know, cock, balls, tits.
jokes.setup: NBC censor Bridget explaining her job censoring inappropriate content
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact recitation of all the crude terms she deals with
jokes.explanation: Good irony of a censor casually listing profanity. The professional detachment from crude language creates amusing contrast.

jokes.text: I can't fight off the penis forever. - Just let it in. - Yeah. Let it just slip right in.
jokes.setup: Bridget explaining her surrender to inevitable crude content
jokes.punchline: Larry's responses creating unintentional sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Obvious double entendre humor. The sexual innuendo is predictable and relies on easy wordplay rather than clever construction.

jokes.text: She holds it like a pencil. That I've never seen before. - That's annoying. - Yeah, and she keeps the window open in the car. - Ugh, air.
jokes.setup: Larry roleplaying describing date flaws to a friend
jokes.punchline: His petty complaints about silverware grip and fresh air preference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry neuroses - finding fault in harmless quirks. The 'ugh, air' response is particularly good at showing his unreasonable standards.

jokes.text: Why is Mom going out with you? - Oh... Yeah, no, that's a very good question, Eddie. Very good question. I don't know.
jokes.setup: Bridget's son Eddie encountering Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's honest agreement with the kid's insulting question
jokes.explanation: Great Larry deflation - his inability to defend himself against a child's honest observation is perfectly self-deprecating.

jokes.text: Your head is so shiny.
jokes.setup: Larry meeting Bridget's son for first time
jokes.punchline: Kid's blunt observation about Larry's bald head
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective kid humor. The brutal honesty about Larry's appearance is predictable but still lands due to timing and context.

jokes.text: Why are these chocolate sprinkles? On the spectrum? - Mm-hmm. - I only eat rainbow sprinkles!
jokes.setup: Revelation that Eddie has Asperger's
jokes.punchline: Eddie's immediate demonstration through rigid sprinkle preferences
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Eddie's condition through specific behavior rather than exposition. The sprinkle fixation is believable and specific.

jokes.text: Because he didn't sound black on the phone, okay?!
jokes.setup: Jeff asking why Larry blurted out surprise about Greg being black
jokes.punchline: Larry's embarrassing explanation about vocal expectations
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry doubling down on inappropriate comments. His inability to let awkward moments pass creates deeper cringe.

jokes.text: Oh, Jesus Christ! What are you doing up here? This is upstairs. You don't come upstairs in somebody's house. - W-why? What's so special about... - This is my boudoir! It's off-limits! Off-limits!
jokes.setup: Larry wandering upstairs at Jeff's house
jokes.punchline: Susie's explosive reaction and dramatic declaration about her 'boudoir'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie explosion with the pretentious 'boudoir' language. Her territorial fury over upstairs access is hilariously disproportionate.

jokes.text: Mrs. Cleaver hated Eddie Haskell 'cause Eddie Haskell was a kiss-ass, obsequious, little shit. - I think he had access to the house. - But that's because the boys were upstairs. - Okay, so all I'm asking for is Haskell-access. I want the same... I want the same privileges as Eddie Haskell! Is that asking too much?!
jokes.setup: Larry arguing about house access using TV references
jokes.punchline: Larry's demand for 'Haskell-access' as a formal privilege category
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation. Larry creating 'Haskell-access' as a formal concept and his passionate defense of this imaginary right is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Has Jeff seen you in those curlers of late? - Get! Out! Out! Out! - Oh, what a fucking asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry making final dig about Susie's appearance
jokes.punchline: Susie's explosive ejection and Larry's parting insult
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry/Susie escalation ending. The curlers comment is a predictable low blow but fits their dynamic.

jokes.text: You might feel like a five, but Uber says you're a one, buddy. - Holy shit. Four other stars next to it, not filled in. - The Romanian! The Romanian. Gave me... he gave me a one and took me down from a five to a one.
jokes.setup: New Uber driver explaining Larry's one-star rating
jokes.punchline: Larry's realization that the Romanian's revenge was devastating
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback payoff showing the Romanian's ultimate revenge. Larry's dramatic fall from five to one star creates real consequences for his behavior.

jokes.text: I can't fuck at 92 degrees in my room 'cause my room won't get hot enough. It gets hot in here.
jokes.setup: Larry asking why Leon is using his house
jokes.punchline: Leon's practical explanation about optimal fucking temperature
jokes.explanation: Excellent Leon logic - his matter-of-fact approach to sexual logistics is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: She's a yoga instructor. She likes hot yoga. - Oh, so she likes hot sex and hot yoga, is that it? - That's fuckin' right. That's what the fuck she does.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining why Larry's house is 92 degrees
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic summary and Leon's proud confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting multiple storylines. Leon's pride in his hot yoga connection creates character-driven humor.

jokes.text: I don't like the scent of fuck, okay? Sue me. I don't like fuck scent.
jokes.setup: Leon defending post-sex presence in Larry's house
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt declaration about disliking sex smell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry neuroticism - turning a normal human function into a personal affront. The 'fuck scent' phrasing is memorably crude and specific.

jokes.text: There's nothing wrong with people having the scent of... of fuck on their body. - I don't like the scent of fuck, okay? Sue me. I don't like fuck scent.
jokes.setup: Leon defending post-coital presence in house
jokes.punchline: Larry's repeated rejection of 'fuck scent'
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Leon's acceptance vs Larry's germaphobia, with repeated 'fuck scent' being funny.

jokes.text: How do you know he's black? - I know how black people sound. - You could tell that guy was black? - Fuck, yeah, instantly. Three words. Bam. Jews, I could tell within, like, a minute. - Man or woman. - Of course, 'cause you're Jewish. You... you give me two minutes, and I could tell you if they're reform or conservative.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning Leon's ability to identify race by voice
jokes.punchline: Leon's detailed breakdown of his vocal profiling abilities, ending with Jewish denominations
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity with Leon's increasingly specific claims about vocal identification. The Jewish denomination finale is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: It's the optimal temperature for climaxing. - Oh, is it? Ha, ha, is it? You... are gonna have to leave. - Such hospitality. - Feels good? Namaste. - Okay. Now get the fuck out. - I'm still gonna nama-stick-it-in-her.
jokes.setup: Tina explaining yoga temperature preferences, Larry kicking them out
jokes.punchline: Leon's 'nama-stick-it-in-her' pun combining namaste with sexual intent
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback pun that perfectly combines the namaste theme with Leon's sexual agenda. The wordplay is crude but cleverly constructed.

jokes.text: Let me just open a programming window on my phone and enter some code for you.
jokes.setup: Larry asking driver to change his Uber rating
jokes.punchline: Driver's sarcastic explanation of why he can't change it
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcasm about technology - the driver's mock-technical explanation is both educational and dismissive.

jokes.text: Let me just open a programming window on my phone and enter some code for you. - What are you writing over there? - I'm just entering some notes for other drivers.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Romanian to change his Uber rating
jokes.punchline: Romanian's sarcastic response about programming, then ominous note-taking
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response showing the Romanian's technical knowledge. The ominous note-taking builds tension effectively.

jokes.text: My sister would not look at a picture of you. - She's a gorgeous Romanian woman. - Gorgeous Romanian? That's an oxymoron. Gorgeous Romanian.
jokes.setup: Romanian defending his sister's beauty
jokes.punchline: Larry's inflammatory declaration that 'gorgeous Romanian' is an oxymoron
jokes.explanation: Larry's inflammatory comment perfectly escalates the conflict. The oxymoron observation is crude but effectively provocative.

jokes.text: My sister would not look at a picture of you. She's a gorgeous Romanian woman. - Gorgeous Romanian? That's an oxymoron.
jokes.setup: Driver defending his sister's beauty
jokes.punchline: Larry's continued insulting of Romanian women with 'oxymoron'
jokes.explanation: Larry doubling down on offensive behavior - the 'oxymoron' usage shows his education used for cruelty.

jokes.text: And I'm telling you, I don't think Bridget's kid has Asperger's. I swear to God, I just think he's an asshole. - The kid's an asshole. - I know a lot of people with Asperger's, I do. Not a lot, but I know some. And you know what, they're delightful people. This kid, I'm betting you're right. - Asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry's theory about Eddie not having Asperger's
jokes.punchline: Leon's agreement and their joint conclusion that the kid is just an asshole
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective character analysis. Larry's distinction between actual Asperger's and being an asshole creates uncomfortable but funny territory.

jokes.text: I don't think Bridget's kid has Asperger's. I swear to God, I just think he's an asshole. - The kid's an asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry analyzing Eddie's behavior
jokes.punchline: Larry's diagnosis that the kid is just an asshole, not on spectrum
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Larry's willingness to call a kid an asshole rather than accept medical explanation.

jokes.text: I'm gonna start telling people that you're on the spectrum. - I could be. Any bad behavior can be written off as what? - Spectrum. - Spectrum.
jokes.setup: Larry's plan to use Asperger's as an excuse
jokes.punchline: Leon's enthusiastic agreement about using the spectrum as a universal excuse
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about exploiting disability for social forgiveness. The cynical recognition of how diagnoses can excuse behavior is uncomfortably accurate.

jokes.text: I suggest you foster a kid. - Okay. I'll go... - It'll make you a better man. - I'll go down to the pound today. I'll go down... I'll go down to the foster pound.
jokes.setup: Jeff suggesting Larry foster a child to understand kids better
jokes.punchline: Larry treating child fostering like getting a pet from the pound
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Larry logic - treating children like shelter animals shows his fundamental misunderstanding of parenting.

jokes.text: Say you had a 'Sophie's Choice.' You have two children, heating and air-conditioning. You have to pick one... and the other one goes into the concentration camp. Which one... which one you gonna keep?
jokes.setup: Larry asking HVAC guy about his preference between heating and AC
jokes.punchline: Larry using Holocaust reference for HVAC preferences
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriate analogy - using Sophie's Choice for mundane HVAC preferences is darkly brilliant and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Say you had a 'Sophie's Choice.' You have two children, heating and air-conditioning. You have to pick one, the one you had an infinity for, and the other one goes into the concentration camp. Which one... which one you gonna keep? - Heat. Go with heat. - So air conditioning's going to the concentration camp.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to determine HVAC guy's specialty preference
jokes.punchline: Escalating to Holocaust metaphor for heating vs air conditioning choice
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry absurdism - turning HVAC preferences into Holocaust metaphor. The escalation from simple question to 'Sophie's Choice' is brilliantly inappropriate and memorable.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna have sex with my shirt off. - You don't take your shirt off during sex? - No, I don't... no. - I take my shirt off while I'm kissing.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he won't have sex in hot room
jokes.punchline: Larry's admission about keeping shirt on during sex vs HVAC guy's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy revealing Larry's body image issues contrasted with HVAC guy's confidence.

jokes.text: White people. - Hey, man, white people. Gotta love 'em. Namaste. - Namaste up in this mother fucker.
jokes.setup: Justin apologizing and overpaying due to racial guilt
jokes.punchline: Leon and Justin bonding over white guilt with namaste blessing
jokes.explanation: Great callback that ironically uses namaste to celebrate white guilt benefits. The street version 'namaste up in this mother fucker' is perfectly Leon.

jokes.text: Thing is that I didn't m... tell you was that I have Asperger's. - You have Asperger's? Ah. - Jeff never mentioned anything, I'm so sorry. - Why would he need to? - Well, no, no, no, I... he doesn't need to. You... you're right. I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming to have Asperger's to excuse his behavior
jokes.punchline: Greg's immediate apology and complete service reversal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback payoff showing Larry's cynical plan working perfectly. The immediate transformation from hostility to accommodation is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Car will be ready when? - Tomorrow. - Tomorrow. - Free. - S-see you tomorrow. Tomorrow. - T-t... yeah, tomorrow. - Tomorrow, tomorrow. - Yeah, okay. - Tomorrow, tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Larry getting free car repair due to fake Asperger's claim
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awkward repetition of 'tomorrow' as Larry milks the situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's fake condition performance. The repetitive speech pattern attempts to sell the Asperger's act.

jokes.text: There must have been 10,000 ejaculations in that place in just two years. - That means that room is broken in. It's ready. It's prime and ready to fuck.
jokes.setup: Larry rejecting the guest house for his date
jokes.punchline: Leon's positive spin on the guest house's sexual history
jokes.explanation: Excellent Leon logic - reframing the guest house's sordid history as a selling point with characteristic confidence.

jokes.text: There must have been 10,000 ejaculations in that place in just two years. - That means that room is broken in. It's ready. It's prime and ready to fuck.
jokes.setup: Larry disgusted by guest house sexual history
jokes.punchline: Leon's enthusiastic reframe as positive sexual preparation
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Larry's disgust vs Leon's practical enthusiasm. The 'broken in' metaphor is crude but effectively funny.

jokes.text: You gonna bring my rating down. I'm a good five right now. I'm a five. - No, no, you a fuckin' one.
jokes.setup: Leon refusing to call Larry an Uber
jokes.punchline: Leon's awareness of Larry's terrible rating
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing even Leon knows about Larry's rating problem.

jokes.text: What are you sitting in the middle for? Why don't you move over one? It's like using the middle urinal.
jokes.setup: Larry on bus with person sitting in middle of row
jokes.punchline: Larry's urinal etiquette comparison
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy - Larry's urinal analogy for bus seating is relatable and well-constructed.

jokes.text: It's like using the middle urinal. - You know it's odd, I... I'm not sure if you can understand me or not just... - No, no, you go over there. - No, you sit there. No, no.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about middle seat etiquette on bus
jokes.punchline: Awkward interaction with non-English speaker leading to seat confusion
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about personal space etiquette. The urinal comparison is apt but the execution is somewhat mundane.

jokes.text: I'm not your babysitter. Figure it out. This bus is a very cold, unforgiving place. I don't like it one bit.
jokes.setup: Larry asking for help on the bus
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic assessment of bus culture
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry observation - his dramatic characterization of bus atmosphere as 'cold and unforgiving' is perfectly overwrought.

jokes.text: I have Asperger's! I'm on the spectrum! - Ah! - Get. - If I see you on my bus again, I'm a fuck you up.
jokes.setup: Bus driver kicking Larry off for causing accident
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate use of fake Asperger's defense failing completely
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the limits of Larry's new excuse strategy. The bus driver's complete indifference to the diagnosis is perfectly realistic.

jokes.text: [Leon and Tina's loud sex sounds from guest house] - Do I hear a Jew? - I'm not gonna do that!
jokes.setup: Leon and Tina having loud sex in guest house
jokes.punchline: Tina's mid-coitus question about hearing Larry, followed by Larry's rejection
jokes.explanation: Good cringe ending - Tina's casual identification of Larry during sex creates perfect uncomfortable finale.

jokes.text: Do I hear a Jew? - I'm not gonna do that!
jokes.setup: Leon having loud sex while Larry returns home
jokes.punchline: Leon's mid-coitus recognition of Larry's presence and Larry's horrified refusal to respond
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe ending with Leon's casual mid-sex Jewish identification and Larry's mortified response. The timing and absurdity are excellent.
